title: Obici House - Carriage House (HABS VA-1438-A, plans sheet 1)
building_type: accessory dwelling / carriage house (garage below, apartment above)
architectural_style: Early-20th-century estate carriage house / garage with upper dwelling
era_or_year: Obici estate (Planters Peanuts founder Amedeo Obici); HABS drawings
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey (HABS VA-1438-A), delineated by Raphael Lister
rightsholder_or_source: Library of Congress HABS (Prints & Photographs Division)
research_notes: Combined sheet: ground-floor garage plan + upper apartment plan + roof plan on sheet 1 of 2. Pairs naturally with the main Obici House (LoC va2129 / HABS VA-1438) to model a multi-building property.
research_features: Fills the known multi-building/accessory-dwelling gap: this is the detached CARRIAGE HOUSE of a larger estate (the Obici House is the main residence, documented separately as VA-1438). One combined sheet shows the GROUND FLOOR PLAN (garage bay with two stairs, water heater, storage), the FIRST FLOOR PLAN (an upstairs apartment: three bedrooms, living room, kitchen, dining, bath, two stairs), and a ROOF PLAN. Good two-stair, mixed-use accessory-building test case.
feature_highlights: detached estate carriage house: garage below, full apartment above
three stacked drawings on one sheet: ground floor, first floor, roof plan
large open garage bay flanked by two stairs and storage rooms
upstairs dwelling with three bedrooms, living, dining, kitchen, and bath
two stairways (Stair 1 with landing, Stair 2) spanning both floors
hip-roofed projecting porch/terrace drawn with diagonal hatch
roof plan with hipped shingle hatch and ridge/hip lines
full imperial dimension strings and per-plan materials notes
supported_examples: named rectangular rooms (bedrooms, kitchen, bath, garage, storage)
roadmap_examples: multi-floor management (two stacked occupied levels plus a roof plan)
straight-run stairs as 2D symbols with floor-spanning topology
north arrow and graphic scale bar as plan annotations

gap_features.why: this is a detached accessory carriage house, one of several structures on the Obici estate whose main residence is documented separately
a self-contained apartment is stacked over a garage in one structure, a small mixed-use dwelling-over-service arrangement beyond the single-residence model
the hip-roofed projecting porch/terrace at the right end is a roofed but open space rather than an enclosed room
the sheet includes a dedicated roof plan of the hipped roof with ridge and hip lines, which is roof geometry not a floor
